title: Method of manufacturing and purifying exosomes from non-terminally differentiated cells
abstract: The present invention discloses a high volume manufacturing process for the production and purification of exosomes. In one embodiment, a hollow fiber perfusion reactor is used to expand stem cells. The growth media used from the stem cell expansion is captured, filtered, centrifuged and exosomes isolated from the waste effluent. In one embodiment, stem cells are derived from bone marrow, fat, blood, cord blood and induced pluripotent stem cells are expanded and the exosomes captured from the growth waste effluent. The exosomes can be used as therapeutics and diagnostics for a number of regenerative and chronic diseases.
claims:
Claims WHAT IS CLAIMED IS: 1. A method of purifying exosomes from non-terminally differentiated cells, comprising: providing a cellular effluent from non-terminally differentiated mammalian cells cultured in media; separating the cellular effluent into the mammalian cells and a first supernatant comprising exosomes; separating the first supernatant into cellular debris and a second supernatant comprising exosomes; filtering the second supernatant into a filtrate comprising exosomes; subjecting the filtrate to ultracentrifugation to pellet the exosomes, resuspending the pelleted exosomes and subjecting the resuspended exosomes to density gradient ultracentrifugation to isopycnically isolate the exosomes; and collecting the isolated exosomes. 2. The method of claim 1 wherein the effluent is a stem cell effluent. 3. The method of claim 1 or 2 wherein the cellular effluent is separated by centrifugation. 4. The method of claim 3 wherein the cellular effluent is subjected to up to about 750 x g or up to about 1000 x g, 5. The method of any one of claims 1 to 4 wherein the first supernatant is separated by centrifugation. 6. The method of claim 5 wherein the first supernatant is subjected to up to about 2000 x g. or up to about 2500 x g 7. The method of any one of claims 1 to 6 wherein the second supernatant is further subjected to centrifugation before filtration. 8. The method of claim 7 wherein the second supernatant is subjected to up 

 to about 10,000 x g or up to about 11, 000 x g before filtration. 9. The method of any one of claims 1 to 8 wherein the filtrate is subjected to up to about 100,000 x g or up to about 200,000 x g. 10. The method of any one of claims 1 to 9 wherein the filter is an about 0.2 to about 0.3 micron filter. 11. The method of any one of claims 1 to 10 wherein the resuspended exosomes are subjected to up to about 100,000 x g or up to about 200,000 x g for about 1 8 hours. 12. The method of any one of claims 1 to 11 further comprising subjected the isolated exosomes to solvent exchange. 13. The method of any one of claims 1 to 12 wherein the effluent is obtained from a hollow fiber bioreactor comprising a plurality of fibers that is part of a sterile closed-loop circuit for media and gas exchange; a gas exchange module; a waste bag; and a cell harvest bag, wherein the fibers are coated with a glycoprotein and a molecule or mixture that promotes cell attachment. 14. The method of any one of claims 1 to 13 wherein the mammalian cells are human cells. 15. The method of any one of claims 1 to 14 wherein the ceil s are neural progenitor cells, induced pluripotent stem ceils, mesenchymal stem cells, embryonic stem cells or hematopoietic stem cells. 16. A composition comprising isolated exosomes prepared by the method of any one of claims 1 to 15. 17. A method of purifying exosomes from mammalian ceils, comprising: providing an isolated supernatant comprising exosomes and lacking cells, 

 which supernatant is obtained from cultured mammalian cells; filtering the supernatant to provide a filtrate comprising exosomes; concentrating the filtrate via ultracentrifugation to provide for isolated exosomes; subjecting the isolated exosomes to density gradient ultracentrifugation to isopycnically isolate the exosomes; and collecting the isolated exosomes. 18. A method of purifying exosomes from mammalian cells, comprising: providing an isolated supernatant comprising exosomes and lacking cells, which supernatant is obtained from cultured mammalian cells; filtering the supernatant to provide a filtrate comprising exosomes; concentrating the filtrate via ultracentrifugation to provide for isolated exosomes or subj ecting filtrate to density gradient ultracentrifugation to isopycnically isolate the exosomes; and collecting the isolated exosomes. 19. The method of claim 17 or 1 8 wherein the cultured cells are non- terminally differentiated cells. 20. The method of any one of claims 17 to 19 wherein the isolated supernatant is provided by separating a cellular effluent from cells and/or cell debris by centrifugation. 21. The method of claim 20 wherein the centrifugation is about 700 to about 800 x g or about 300 to about 1000 x g. 22. The method of claim 20 or 21 wherein the cellular effluent is separated from cells and/or cell debris by centrifugation from about 1500 to about 2500 x g. 23. The method of any one of claims 17 to 23 wherein the fi ltrate i s subjected to centrifugation up to about 100,000 x g or up to about 200,000 x g. 24. The method of any one of claims 17 to 23 wherein the filter is an about 0. 15 to an about 0.3 micron filter. 25. The method of any one of claims 20 to 24 wherein the effluent is obtained from a hollow fiber bioreactor comprising a plurality of fibers that is part of a sterile closed-loop circuit for media and gas exchange; a gas exchange module; a waste bag; and a ceil harvest bag, wherein the fibers are coated with a glycoprotein and a molecule or mixture that promotes cell attachment. 26. The method of any one of claims 17 to 25 wherein the mammalian cells are human cells. 27. The method of any one of claims 17 to 26 wherein the cells are neural progenitor cells, induced pluripotent stem ceils, mesenchymal stem cells, embryoni c stem cel ls or hematopoietic stem cells.
